CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-12-2025 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-21-2025 Continued To Oct-08-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-21-2025 Off Calendar

Amended DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-09-2025 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT PETER FITZPATRICK's DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Peter Fitzpatrick's demurrer to the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Raymond Raymond is sustained without leave to amend (see word minutes for complete ruling).

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-07-2025 Continued To Aug-06-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-09-2025 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

Apperances:
Plaintiff RAYMOND RAYMOND, in pro per present (tel:415-860-8980).
No other appearances made in this matter.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT PETER FITZPATRICK's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

Matter discussed as to the defects in Plaintiff's notice of contest. The Court does not allow Plaintiff to present argument against the tentative ruling. Upon conclusion, the Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Peter Fitzpatrick's unopposed request for judicial notice is granted. The demurrer is sustained with leave to amend.

Plaintiff Raymond Raymond brings this legal malpractice action against his former attorney, Fitzpatrick, who represented him in criminal and competency proceedings. According to the complaint, the representation began in April 2018. (Compl. at 1:23-24.) According to the Register of Actions in Raymond's criminal case, a different attorney substituted in to represent Raymond on April 8, 2019. (Ex. 37 to Thurston Dec.) Raymond states that he was freed in 2019. (Compl. at 3:19.) His complaint appears to allege negligence in Fitzpatrick's handling of his competency proceedings, although the basis for the malpractice claim is not clear.

A demurrer tests the sufficiency of the complaint on its face and "accept[s] the truth of material facts properly pleaded in the operative complaint." (Capito v. San Jose Healthcare System, LP (2024) 17 Cal.5th 273 [internal quotation marks and citation omitted].) The court may also consider matters judicially noticeable but may not resolve disputed facts. (See Fremont Indemnity Co. v. Fremont General Corp. (2007) 148 Cal.App.4th 97, 115.)

The statute of limitations for a legal malpractice action is one year from when a plaintiff discovers or should have discovered the wrongful act or omission, and no more than four years from when the act or omission occurred, unless the plaintiff alleges actual fraud by the attorney. (Code Civ. Proc. 340.6(a).) Here, it appears that any acts or omissions by Fitzpatrick in connection with Raymond's case occurred on or before April 8, 2019, the date he was relieved as counsel, which is more than four years before the September 3, 2024 filing of the complaint. On its face, then, the action is barred by the statute of limitations. Raymond does not allege any facts that occurred within the one-year limitations period, nor does he allege facts supporting actual fraud that would extend the limitations period to four years.

Generally leave to amend must be granted unless it is clear from the complaint that the defect cannot be cured. Here, Raymond is proceeding pro se and may have facts to allege that would put this case within the four-year limitations period for a malpractice action arising out of actual fraud or the one-year limitations period for all other attorney malpractice actions. The court grants Raymond leave to amend within 20 days of notice of entry of this order.

Order signed.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
